title: Unwrap on regex compile and capture groups in expand_file_refs risks runtime panic
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
error-handling
## Location
src/tui/app.rs:134, 142-143
## Violation
Three unconditional `unwrap()` calls in a hot user-input path. The TUI is a long-running interactive process — a panic here tears down the alt-screen with raw mode enabled and leaves the user's terminal in a broken state.

```rust
let re = regex::Regex::new(r#"@"([^"]+)"|@(\S+)"#).unwrap();
...
let full_match = cap.get(0).unwrap().as_str();
let raw_path = cap.get(1).or_else(|| cap.get(2)).unwrap().as_str();
```

`cap.get(1).or_else(|| cap.get(2))` is also load-bearing — if the regex is ever changed to introduce additional groups, the unwrap may fire on a `None`. The regex itself is recompiled on every call (regex compilation is expensive — ~100us-1ms per call).

## Mandated refactor
1. Lift the regex to a `once_cell::sync::Lazy<Regex>` / `std::sync::LazyLock` constant so it compiles exactly once and any compile error surfaces at first use (which can be tested in unit tests).
2. Replace `unwrap()` on capture groups with explicit handling: skip the iteration with `continue` if the expected group is absent. The first call (`cap.get(0)`) is safe per regex contract but should still use `expect("regex Match must have group 0")` to document the invariant rather than swallow the diagnostic.

## Filed from QA review dispatch
